c74object.name: dk.concatsynth~
c74object.description: The dk.concatplayer~ object creates real-time audio mosaicking via concatenative synthesis with a pre-analyzed concat corpus. dk.concatsynth~ handles the audio analysis and nearest neighbor matching in a single object.
c74object.discussion:
If you want to learn more about the underlying algorithm have a look at this article:
https://learn.flucoma.org/reference/kdtree

inlet.description: Audio input to be analyzed for concatenation/resynthesis.

outlet.digest: Position and name outlet for waveform~
outlet.description: Sends the buffer~ name and playback position to be used with waveform~.

objarg.name: default
objarg.digest: JSON file to load by default
objarg.description: JSON file to load by default.

method.name: info
method.digest: Print summary information about the concat corpus
method.description: Print summary information about the concat corpus.

method.name: print
method.digest: Print summary info to Max console
method.description: Prints summary info including overall statistics to the Max console.

method.name: read
method.description: Reads a pre-trained concat .json file directly from a relative or absolute path. With no additional argument, a file dialog will appear to choose a .json file.

method.name: refer
method.description:
Messages related to buffer~ loading and referring.
You can point the analysis to an existing buffer~ by sending sending a refer message followed by a valid buffer~ name. This will immediately analyze the named buffer~ and make it available for playback as soon as the process is done.
Additional optional arguments allow you to specify a subsection of the buffer~ to load for analysis.
A single additional argument will specify how much of the buffer~ (in ms) to analyze from the start. If two arguments are given these will specify the start position (in ms) of the analysis and then the duration (in ms) of the buffer~ to analyze after that point.
Therefore a complete message would look as follows:
refer bufferName or refer bufferName 500 or refer bufferName 500 1500

attribute.name: loudness
attribute.digest: Amount of loudness compensation to apply
attribute.description: Amount of loudness compensation to apply (bewteen -100 and 100%).

attribute.name: neighbors
attribute.digest: The number of neighbors to return
attribute.description: The number of neighbors to return.

attribute.name: pitch
attribute.digest: Amount of pitch compensation to apply
attribute.description: Amount of pitch compensation to apply (between -100 and 100%).

attribute.name: radius
attribute.description: The maximum distance (in high dimensional space) that a returned point can be. Any points beyond radius will not be returned (even if they're within the nearest neighbors points). When radius is 0, it is no longer a constraint and the distance of a nearest neighbor is not taken into account.

attribute.name: random
attribute.description: Enable random variation to the matching/concatenation.

attribute.name: rate
attribute.description: Rate of matching/playback in the concatenation engine (in %).

attribute.name: size
attribute.digest: Size of grain to play back
attribute.description: Size of grain to play back, in %.

attribute.name: steal
attribute.digest: Whether poly voice stealing is enabled
attribute.description: Sets whether voice stealing is enabled, which allows new samples to start when previous ones are still sounding.

attribute.name: speed
attribute.description: Sets the playback speed for each individual grain.

attribute.name: weights
attribute.description:
Messages related to descriptor weighting.
After sending the weights message you send a series of additional arguments that determine what descriptor is being weighted, and how much its weight is being modified by.
The available descriptors that can be used for weighting are:
loudness
loudness_derivative
centroid
centroid_derivative
flatness
flatness_derivative
pitch
pitch_confidence
The descriptors are weighted with a percentage between 0% and 1000%, with 100% being the default.
Therefore a complete message would look as follows:
weights loudness 200 or weights pitch 1000 or weights flatness 50, weights pitch_confidence 500

attribute.name: width
attribute.description: Sets the random distribution width for the grain panning.

attribute.name: window
attribute.description: Sets the window function for the grain playback." The available options are: sine hann triangle rampdown rampup onset hamming blackman square

attribute.name: voices
attribute.description: Sets the amount of polyphony available for sample playback.
